Off Calendar Oct-21-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via CourtCall:

Emily M. Cunningham of HAWKINS PARNELL & YOUNG (415-766-3200) appearing for Intervenor Marconi Plastering Company, Inc.;
Solomon Pantuch of HAWKINS PARNELL & YOUNG (415-766-3200) appearing for defendant Triple A Machine Shop, Inc.;

Elspeth V. Hansen of Mayer Brown LLP (650-331-2000), appearing for defendant Foster Poultry Farms;
Margaret F. Mahaffey of WFBM, LLP (714-634-2522), appearing for defendant D. Zelinsky & Sons, Inc.

Ruling:
Not contested and the Court adopted its tentative ruling:
Plaintiffs' Motion for Order Granting Preference in Setting Case for Trial, and Extending Discovery Cutoff is GRANTED under Code Civil Procedure section 36(a). Plaintiffs have shown that Rosemarie Waining's health is such that a preference is necessary to prevent prejudicing her interest in the litigation. The Court did not consider the opposition by D. Zelinsky & Sons, Inc. as it was untimely filed. (Code Civ. Proc. Section 1001(b).)

The trial date is February 7, 2022 at 11:15 a.m. in Department 503. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. section 36(f) is February 18, 2022. Local Rule 20 shall be followed by the parties as well as all San Francisco Local Rules and California Rules of Court.

The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is February 3, 2022. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P., unless the parties stipulate otherwise. The Court will allow a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared in this action. Before a party files and serves such motion, it must contact the Court's Calendar Clerk to make a reservation. Time to respond to written discovery that has not yet been served is shortened to 20 days. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service. Fact discovery cut-off date is January 21, 2022. Expert discovery cut-off date is February 4, 2022. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than November 19, 2021. If Plaintiffs submit documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, they must notify defendants of this no later than five days after submission. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents.

RULING:
Hearing held, argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant D. Zelinsky & Sons, Inc.'s Motion to Continue the Trial Date is DENIED. Defendant failed to show good cause under CRC 3.1332(c) or (d). That a motion for summary judgment is not scheduled or heard before trial is not grounds for continuing the trial date. (Local Rule 6(B).) Notwithstanding the lack of good cause, Defendant does not address the parties' stipulation filed March 12, 2020, which states that the August 10, 2020 trial date does not allow for dispositive motions, including summary judgment motions, to be filed by defendants whose deadline passed pursuant to the previous trial date. (Renken Declaration, Ex. B.) Defendant also does not address Plaintiffs' agreement to shorten time on the motion for summary judgment if the papers remained the same, given that Defendant already had an opportunity to file a motion for summary judgment. (Renken Declaration, Ex. C.) Defendant's log submitted as proof that courtesy copies of the motion were delivered to the Court is inadequate as the log states that copies were delivered to the 9th District, not Department 503. Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Reporter: Janice H. Dispo of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #13299, jdispo.csr@gmail.com (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/CML)
